agree_with: information-designer's double-encoding observation: freshest row carries Tier-1 bold AND a `●` gutter — same signal twice. I noted the 4-primitive ceiling but missed naming it as redundant ink. Tufte 'is this ink earning its keep?' rejects it.
Both panelists' '7/min' redundancy point: the suffix label restates what the sparkline shape shows. Drop the label, the row is calmer and the shape does the work.

information-designer's framing that the verb-glyph column IS the design's small-multiples instrument — and that an unclosed dictionary breaks small-multiples discipline by definition (some rows carry shape, others don't, eye cannot read column directly). This sharpens my own 'risk over a week of use' note into a structural argument.
challenge: implementation-realist treats sparkline data wiring as a hud-fidelity defect. I read it as terminal-feasibility (the rendered primitive is sound; whether the producer emits a usable bucketable stream is implementability). Holding the concern, scoring it under feasibility.
concede: double-encoding and '7/min' redundancy are real Tufte hits I underweighted. Score holds at 7 because both panelists landed at 7 with these same concerns — the rationale tightens, the number doesn't move.
hud-fidelity.revised_rationale: Both terminal tests pass — sparkline (≤8 cells fixed alphabet inside an existing line) and spellcheck (gutter `●` and end-of-row `✗` on the line of work). Three Tufte-grade primitive-economy concerns now consolidated across panelists: (1) freshest is double-encoded as Tier-1 bold AND `●` gutter — the same signal carried twice, redundant ink. (2) `7/min` suffix restates what the sparkline shape shows. (3) Per-row primitive count on the freshest row is at the rubric ceiling (gutter + verb-glyph + bold + end-row badge = 4). The deeper issue is the verb-glyph column: it is the design's small-multiples instrument, but if the dictionary has gaps (sketch shows 5, Q4 admits non-tool events have no glyph, fallback unresolved), column 1 carries shape on some rows and is blank on others — and small-multiples discipline collapses the moment the column is inconsistent. Eyebrow rows spend a whole row on a 7-char source string, which under multi-source bursts is a low-data-ink trade. Bones are right; the per-row primitive ceiling, the duplicated freshest encoding, and dictionary closure are the open edges.

agree_with: Both panelists' stuck-vs-idle ambiguity: a flat `▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁` is generated by both 'nothing happening' and 'agent is stuck waiting'. These are completely different states for a watcher and the spec cannot tell them apart at a glance. I missed this — it's a real Pattern hole, not just an Exception edge.

information-designer's gutter-mass alternative for Exception. With `✗` at row-end, errors don't accumulate visually as a column the eye can count. Moving the failure mark left into a gutter slot would give canonical Tufte 'count by mass without reading' — strictly better for the error-heavy state.
challenge: implementation-realist's 7. The stuck-vs-idle issue is real but Pattern is still answerable by glance for any active flow (which is the common case). Three-strong-and-one-edge-weak still merits 8 in my reading. Holding at 8.
concede: My phase-1 named only Exception as the weakest question. Both panelists correctly identify Pattern as also contingent (stuck-vs-idle). Updating weakest to a tie between Pattern and Exception.
four-questions.revised_rationale: All four answerable by glance: Liveness from sparkline existence, Pattern from sparkline shape, Exception from row-end `✗` plus tone, Latest from `●` gutter. Two contingencies the panel agrees on: (1) Pattern conflates 'stuck' (recent-but-unchanging) with 'idle' (no events) — both render as flat `▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁`, a watcher-critical distinction the design can't make at a glance. (2) Exception is encoded as an end-of-row glyph rather than as gutter mass; under error-heavy load a left-column accumulation of `✗` would let the eye count failures without reading them, which the current placement forfeits. Latest is also over-encoded (gutter `●` + Tier-1 bold). 4-of-4 by glance, with Pattern and Exception both carrying real edges.

agree_with: implementation-realist's codepoint-width audit: `●` `✓` `✗` are East Asian Width Ambiguous — in CJK locales or terminals with Ambiguous-as-Wide (default in many iTerm2 setups), they render as 2 cells, silently breaking column alignment in the gutter and the end-of-row badge. `⚠` is unconditionally WIDE. I missed this entirely — it is a HUD-ruining defect at narrow widths and exactly the kind of edge this lens grades.

implementation-realist on verb-glyph classification source: `logs[]` entries carry no verb tag, so the renderer would regex-classify message strings client-side. That's a fragility the spec doesn't acknowledge.

implementation-realist's enumeration of unresolved contracts (sparkline data source / bucket geometry / refresh, multi-source vertical budget, system-tagged source eyebrow rule, outcome × truncation, unfocus for non-text glyphs, no four-state mocks). This is the same list I summarised more tersely; theirs is the better catalogue.
challenge: information-designer's 7 ('implementable in principle'). This lens is explicitly graded on behaviour across all four states, narrow widths, unfocus, and a week of use — not on principle. The candidate hand-waves three of the four states, doesn't draw narrow-30, defers unfocus consistency to Q10, and leaves the verb-glyph dictionary open. 'Per-glyph attainable' ≠ 'survives the edges'. Holding at 5.
concede: Codepoint-width is a genuine feasibility hit I omitted. Adding it sharpens the rationale without moving the score, since I was already at 5 for orthogonal reasons (state coverage, multi-source thrash, unfocus drift, dictionary closure).
terminal-feasibility.revised_rationale: Implementable in OpenTUI in principle. The lens grades behaviour across edges, and the candidate hand-waves most of them: (1) Multi-source: eyebrow approach explicitly acknowledged 'expensive' under interleaving (Q5); two agents bursting every 1–2 entries spend 30–50% of a 5–7-row budget on eyebrow rows. (2) Error-heavy: not mocked; `✗` collides with the pre-truncation `…` at the same column-region (Q8 unresolved). (3) Empty: undecided — a flat `▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁ 0/min` would falsely imply a live instrument when nothing is happening, worse-than-current at the 200ms glance. (4) Narrow ~30 cols: not drawn; sparkline fits but rows leave only ~24 cols for description after gutter+verb-glyph+badge. (5) Unfocus: Q10 explicitly defers whether sparkline/verb glyphs slide with tiers — this lens penalises focus/unfocus drift by name. (6) Codepoint width (panel addition): `●` `✓` `✗` are East Asian Width Ambiguous and render as 2 cells in some terminals/locales, breaking gutter alignment; `⚠` is unconditionally WIDE. None of these are nerd-font codepoints with deterministic width. (7) Verb-glyph classification has no producer-side source; renderer would regex-classify, which the dictionary-closure problem then compounds. Single mockup is good; the candidate names most of the right risks but resolves none of them.

asi_candidate.title: Pin the four states with mockups, resolve eyebrow-vs-inline as a hybrid source-run rule, and consolidate exception encoding into the gutter
asi_candidate.summary: A single focused refinement: replace the lone single-source mockup with four 30-col mocked states (empty, single-source, multi-source, error-heavy), resolve open question #5 with a hybrid source-position rule keyed on source-run length, and drop the end-of-row outcome badge in favour of a gutter-displacement rule that gives the lens-correct vertical-stripe behaviour under error-heavy load. Same theme as phase-1 (close the edges the candidate hand-waves), now also closes Pattern's stuck-vs-idle ambiguity, removes the freshest-row double-encoding, and pins codepoint width.
concrete_changes: Add a `## States` section with four 30-col ASCII mockups: empty, single-source steady, multi-source interleaving (pi/cc alternating every 1–2 entries), error-heavy (3+ failed rows). Every state drawn at exactly 30 cols, both focused and unfocused.

Resolve open question #5 with a source-run-length rule: if the same source holds for ≥2 consecutive rows, render eyebrow once and omit it from rows below; if sources interleave (current ≠ previous AND previous ≠ source-before-previous), suppress eyebrows entirely and render a 3-cell column-0 chip `pi│` / `cc│` on each row. This collapses multi-source from N eyebrow rows to 0 at the cost of 3 chars of left-margin width.

Resolve open question #7: empty state = `(no recent activity)` Tier-4 only; the sparkline row is suppressed entirely so a quiet panel never falsely implies a live instrument at the 200ms glance. Distinguish 'stuck' from 'idle' by appending a relative-age suffix to the eyebrow line on the freshest source whenever latest event > 60s old (`pi db92 · 2m ago`) — gives stuck a different shape from idle without adding a primitive.

Resolve open question #8 by consolidation: drop the end-of-row `✓`/`✗` badge entirely. Outcome=failed displaces the verb-glyph in column 1 with the existing severity `✗` (or `nf-md-alert-circle`); outcome=success leaves column 1 as the verb-glyph (success is the unmarked default). This gives the gutter-mass behaviour information-designer flagged (a column of `✗` accumulates visually under error-heavy load), removes the end-of-row × truncation collision, and drops the freshest-row primitive count from 4 → 3.

Remove the freshest-row double-encoding: keep `●` gutter, drop Tier-1 bold on the description (Tier-2 default). One marker for freshest, not two. Drop the `7/min` suffix on the sparkline — the shape carries the rate, the label restates it.

Resolve open question #10 with one explicit line: sparkline glyphs and verb glyphs slide with tiers on unfocus (they are decorative text, not severity); severity-bypass colours and the Clawd identity glyph keep full intensity. Source chips slide with tiers.

Close the verb-glyph dictionary at exactly 5 entries (read / list / search / edit / run) and specify the fallback: any event without a verb mapping renders column 1 *blank* — never a generic `▶`, never a status glyph in the verb slot (status glyphs displace by the outcome rule above, not by overload). Specify producer-side classification: the runtime tags each `logs[]` entry with `verb?: 'read'|'list'|'search'|'edit'|'run'` so the renderer doesn't regex-classify client-side.

Pin codepoint width: replace East Asian Width Ambiguous `●` with a width-deterministic alternative (e.g. nerd-font `nf-md-circle-medium` or `nf-fa-circle`), and forbid `⚠` (WIDE) anywhere in the activity zone. Any new glyph must be entered in `vocab.ts` with an explicit `cellWidth: 1` assertion.
asi_candidate.why_this_lens: Every change closes an edge the lens grades on: multi-source thrash, empty-state honesty, error-heavy gutter-mass, focus/unfocus consistency, dictionary memorability, and now also stuck-vs-idle disambiguation, freshest double-encoding, and codepoint-width determinism at narrow widths. None of them touch the candidate's strong core (sparkline + gutter + small-multiples). The refinement is purely about surviving the states the candidate currently hand-waves.

expected_score_lift.hud-fidelity: +1 (drops freshest double-encoding and `7/min` redundancy; consolidates outcome into gutter so primitive ceiling falls 4 → 3 on the worst row; closes verb-glyph dictionary so column-1 small-multiples discipline holds)
expected_score_lift.four-questions: +0 to +1 (idle/stuck disambiguation closes the Pattern edge; gutter-mass `✗` closes the Exception edge; all four answerable by glance without contingencies)
expected_score_lift.terminal-feasibility: +3 to +4 (the four states drawn, unfocus rule pinned, codepoint width pinned, source-run rule resolves multi-source thrash, producer-side verb tagging removes client-side regex fragility — exactly the gates this score hangs on)

panel_synthesis_notes.convergence: All three panels land at 7/{7,8}/5–7 with substantially overlapping concerns: per-row primitive ceiling, freshest double-encoding, sparkline contract gaps, verb-glyph dictionary closure, four-state mock absence, unfocus rule absence. The candidate's strong core is unchallenged across panels.
panel_synthesis_notes.divergence: Information-designer scored terminal-feasibility 7 against the other two at 5. The split traces to lens scope: per-glyph attainability vs survives-the-edges. For the terminal-ux-pragmatist lens specifically, edges are the assignment; 5 holds.
panel_unique_contributions_i_adopted: implementation-realist: East-Asian-Width-Ambiguous codepoint audit and verb-classification-needs-a-producer observation
both: stuck-vs-idle Pattern ambiguity and `7/min` redundancy
